text.title: growth-tiktok-basics Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-tiktok-basics. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use a Creator account at launch; switch to Business only after establishing consistent posting.
rule.rationale: Creator accounts receive better algorithmic distribution. Business accounts add analytics but reduce organic reach in early phases.

rule.statement: Bio must contain: what you do + who it is for + one CTA word, under 80 characters.
rule.rationale: Viewers decide in 2 seconds whether to follow; a vague bio converts at 1/3 the rate of a specific one.

rule.statement: Display name must include a searchable keyword (e.g., "SaaS Tips | Mike", not just "Mike").
rule.rationale: TikTok search indexes display names; keyword inclusion drives discoverability.

rule.statement: Define 3-4 content pillars before posting. Each pillar must map to an ICP pain point.
rule.rationale: Random content confuses the algorithm's audience-matching; consistent pillars signal which For You Pages to distribute on.

rule.statement: Write the hook (0-3 seconds) before the rest of the script; if the hook is weak, discard the script.
rule.rationale: Hook quality is the single highest-leverage variable; a great body with a weak hook produces zero views.

rule.statement: Always script on-screen text alongside spoken text — they may differ for emphasis.
rule.rationale: Approximately 70% of TikTok is watched without sound; captions are not optional.

rule.statement: Constrain hook to 8-12 spoken words; constrain each body point to 15-25 words.
rule.rationale: Generated and improvised scripts run too long; explicit word-count constraints prevent watch-time collapse.

rule.statement: Post 1 video every day for the first 4 weeks before increasing frequency.
rule.rationale: Daily posting builds algorithm familiarity. Skipping days in the first 30 days resets distribution testing.

rule.statement: Provide the agent with 3-5 real high-performing videos in your niche as format references before requesting scripts.
rule.rationale: Without format references, agents produce structurally correct but tonally generic scripts that underperform platform-native content.
